src_embedding_text: source: design test case id: tc-registration-001 title: successful user registration with otp verification module: user registration source files or pages: 3 covered methods: preconditions: - user has internet access. - email service (e.g., gmail) is functional. - database tables (`users`, `personal_access_tokens`, `jobs`) are empty or contain no conflicting data. test data: - valid email: `test.user@example.com` - valid password: `securepass123!` - otp: `123456` (simulated) test steps: 1. user opens app and clicks "register." 2. user fills form with `test.user@example.com`, `securepass123!`, and submits. 3. app validates email/password and sends `doregistration()` to server. 4. server saves user data to `database` and generates otp (`generateotp()`). 5. server sends otp via email (`sendotp()`) and saves it to `database`. 6. user receives email with otp and enters `123456` in the app. 7. app sends otp to server for verification (`checkotp()`). 8. server validates otp and sends authentication token (`sendauthtoken()`). 9. app saves token and opens home screen. expected result: - user account created in `database` with hashed password. - otp record exists in `database` (later deleted after use). - auth token stored in `app` (e.g., `personal_access_tokens` table). - home screen loads successfully. unit: project:
